generatedSentence: On a rare weekend, the grizzled waiter, a lifelong workaholic, felt a deep spiritual wound. He whiffed the stale air of his city apartment, longing for a true wonderland. His relentless routine was weakening his resolve, prompting him to write cynical poetry. He dreamed of a roaring waterfall and the distant tap of a woodpecker, whatever it took to shed his wicked urban shell, a metaphorical whip lashing at his soul as he stared at his drab wardrobe. The incessant weekday grind had grown unbearable. He sought the warmest escape, recalling ancient tales of a witchy elder practicing powerful witchcraft. He once envisioned himself a noble warrior, now he longed for genuine peace. A faint aroma of tangerine filled his mind, a nearly utopian vision that began to acidify his urban anxieties. Packing an old undershirt and fresh underwear, he boarded a train, heading for a hidden sanctuary, a personal terraria where he hoped to rediscover his lost knighthood. Deep within the pristine forest, under a canopy of shimmering stars, he felt an overwhelming joy. A strange energy wave caused a slight overheating sensation on his forehead. He saw a figure, vaguely human, vested in intricate, organic patterns, offering him a peculiar milk that seemed to mottle with internal light. He had taken his vitamins before this journey, and now felt a wearable sense of empowerment, almost victorious in this mystical encounter. This great being, free from the unjust strictures of old societies, spoke of old spirits, not devils, but guides. It explained its role as a spiritual developer, helping new immigrants to this sacred land. He knew now this was no deadly delusion, but a profound awakening. He no longer feared the lactose in the mystical milk, nor the strange beluga creature he'd glimpsed earlier. With a new sense of owning his own inner acre, he donned the symbolic overcoat and hung the dreamcatcher on his new home's knocker, ready to face the world transformed.
panelSentences: On a rare weekend, the grizzled waiter, a lifelong workaholic, felt a deep spiritual wound. He whiffed the stale air of his city apartment, longing for a true wonderland. His relentless routine was weakening his resolve, prompting him to write cynical poetry. He dreamed of a roaring waterfall and the distant tap of a woodpecker, whatever it took to shed his wicked urban shell, a metaphorical whip lashing at his soul as he stared at his drab wardrobe.

The incessant weekday grind had grown unbearable. He sought the warmest escape, recalling ancient tales of a witchy elder practicing powerful witchcraft. He once envisioned himself a noble warrior, now he longed for genuine peace. A faint aroma of tangerine filled his mind, a nearly utopian vision that began to acidify his urban anxieties. Packing an old undershirt and fresh underwear, he boarded a train, heading for a hidden sanctuary, a personal terraria where he hoped to rediscover his lost knighthood.

Deep within the pristine forest, under a canopy of shimmering stars, he felt an overwhelming joy. A strange energy wave caused a slight overheating sensation on his forehead. He saw a figure, vaguely human, vested in intricate, organic patterns, offering him a peculiar milk that seemed to mottle with internal light. He had taken his vitamins before this journey, and now felt a wearable sense of empowerment, almost victorious in this mystical encounter.

This great being, free from the unjust strictures of old societies, spoke of old spirits, not devils, but guides. It explained its role as a spiritual developer, helping new immigrants to this sacred land. He knew now this was no deadly delusion, but a profound awakening. He no longer feared the lactose in the mystical milk, nor the strange beluga creature he'd glimpsed earlier. With a new sense of owning his own inner acre, he donned the symbolic overcoat and hung the dreamcatcher on his new home's knocker, ready to face the world transformed.
rawPanelSentences: On a rare **weekend**, the grizzled **waiter**, a lifelong **workaholic**, felt a deep spiritual **wound**. He **whiffed** the stale air of his city apartment, longing for a true **wonderland**. His relentless routine was **weakening** his resolve, prompting him to **write** cynical poetry. He dreamed of a roaring **waterfall** and the distant tap of a **woodpecker**, **whatever** it took to shed his **wicked** urban shell, a metaphorical **whip** lashing at his soul as he stared at his drab **wardrobe**.

The incessant **weekday** grind had grown unbearable. He sought the **warmest** escape, recalling ancient tales of a **witchy** elder practicing powerful **witchcraft**. He once envisioned himself a noble **warrior**, now he longed for genuine peace. A faint **aroma** of **tangerine** filled his mind, a nearly **utopian** vision that began to **acidify** his urban anxieties. Packing an old **undershirt** and fresh **underwear**, he boarded a **train**, heading for a hidden sanctuary, a personal **terraria** where he hoped to rediscover his lost **knighthood**.

Deep within the **pristine forest**, under a canopy of shimmering **stars**, he felt an overwhelming **joy**. A strange energy **wave** caused a slight **overheating** sensation on his **forehead**. He saw a figure, **vaguely** human, **vested** in intricate, organic patterns, offering him a peculiar **milk** that seemed to **mottle** with internal light. He had taken his **vitamins** before this journey, and now felt a **wearable** sense of empowerment, almost **victorious** in this mystical encounter.

This **great** being, free from the **unjust** strictures of old societies, spoke of old spirits, not **devils**, but guides. It explained its role as a spiritual **developer**, helping new **immigrants** to this sacred land. He knew now this was no **deadly** delusion, but a profound awakening. He no longer feared the **lactose** in the mystical milk, nor the strange **beluga** creature he'd glimpsed earlier. With a new sense of **owning** his own inner **acre**, he donned the symbolic **overcoat** and hung the **dreamcatcher** on his new home's **knocker**, ready to face the world transformed.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, neo-noir graphic novel style, thick white gutters, clean black borders. A middle-aged man with a weathered face, a neatly trimmed beard, and noticeable crow's feet around his eyes, wearing a worn leather jacket. TOP-LEFT PANEL: The weathered man, in an undershirt, sits slumped at a worn table, staring blankly at a single potted plant on his window sill, from a low-angle shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: The same man, now wearing a simple overcoat and carrying a backpack, steps onto a vintage train, his determined profile visible, captured in a medium shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The man stands with a look of awe in a glowing, pristine forest, accepting a small, ornate cup from an ethereal, adult female figure with flowing gray hair and intricate tattoos, depicted in a wide shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The transformed man, with a subtle glow in his eyes, hangs a detailed dreamcatcher on a rustic wooden door with an ornate knocker, seen from a high-angle shot overlooking a vast acre of land. cel-shading, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting. All humans are visibly adult with mature features. No text, no speech bubbles, no dialogue, no children.

definitions.definition: An injury, such as a cut, stab, or tear, to a (usually external) part of the body.
A hurt to a person's feelings, reputation, prospects, etc.
definitions.example: It took a long time to get over the wound of that insult.

definitions.definition: An injury to a person by which the skin is divided or its continuity broken.
To hurt or injure (someone) by cutting, piercing, or tearing the skin.
definitions.example: The police officer wounded the suspect during the fight that ensued.

definitions.definition: To hurt (a person's feelings).
definitions.example: The actor's pride was wounded when the leading role went to his rival.

definitions.definition: People who are wicked.
Evil or mischievous by nature.
Excellent; awesome; masterful.
definitions.example: That was a wicked guitar solo, bro!

definitions.definition: Very, extremely.
definitions.example: The band we went to see the other night was wicked loud!

definitions.definition: The break in the working week, usually two days including the traditional holy or sabbath day. Thus in western countries, Saturday and Sunday.
To spend the weekend.
definitions.example: We'll weekend at the beach.

definitions.definition: Of, relating to or for the weekend.
definitions.example: I'm wearing my weekend shoes.

definitions.definition: Occurring at the weekend.
definitions.example: a weekend break

definitions.definition: A person who feels compelled to work excessively.
definitions.example: All workaholics should move to Hong Kong, where working hours average over 50 per week.

definitions.definition: A male or female attendant who serves customers at their tables in a restaurant, café or similar.
definitions.example: Waiter! There's a fly in my soup.

definitions.definition: Someone who waits for somebody or something; a person who is waiting.
A person working as an attendant at the London Stock Exchange.
A vessel or tray on which something is carried, as dishes, etc.; a salver. (See etymology of dumbwaiter.)
A custom house officer; a tide waiter.
A watchman.
To waft.
To sniff.
To strike out.
To miss the ball completely.
To attempt to strike and miss, especially being off-balance/vulnerable after missing.
To throw out in whiffs; to consume in whiffs; to puff.
To carry or convey by a whiff, or as by a whiff; to puff or blow away.
To have or give off a strong, unpleasant smell.
To fail spectacularly at a task.
To fish with a handline.
Any bird of many-species subfamily Picinae, with a sharp beak suitable for pecking holes in wood.
Type 92 heavy machine gun
An imaginary or real place full of wonder or marvels.
A flow of water over the edge of a cliff.
A waterfall-like outpouring of liquid, smoke, etc.
definitions.example: A waterfall of mist came from the open freezer.

definitions.definition: Waterfall model
The action of drinking from a vessel without touching it with the lips, considered more sanitary for a shared vessel.
definitions.example: Hey man, can I take a waterfall from your bottle?

definitions.definition: A necktie.
A chignon.
A beard.
definitions.example: I found home a dreary place after my long absence; for half the children I had known were now wearing whiskers or waterfalls... (Mark Twain, Roughing It)

definitions.definition: To fall like a waterfall.
To drink (something) from a container by pouring it from a height so as not to touch one's lips to the rim.
A room for keeping clothes and armor safe, particularly a dressing room or walk-in closet beside a bedroom.
A governmental office or department in a monarchy which purchases, keeps, and cares for royal clothes.
The building housing such a department.
Any closet used for storing anything.
A room for keeping costumes and other property safe at a theater; a prop room.
The department of a theater, movie studio, etc which purchases, keeps, and cares for costumes; its staff; its room(s) or building(s).
A movable cupboard or cabinet designed for storing clothes, particularly as a large piece of bedroom furniture.
A tall built-in cupboard or closet for storing clothes, often including a rail for coat-hangers, and usually located in a bedroom.
Anything that similarly stores or houses something.
The contents of a wardrobe: an individual's entire collection of clothing.
Any collection of clothing.
Any collection of anything.
A private chamber, particularly one used for sleeping or urinating and defecating.
Badger feces, particularly used in tracking game.
To act as a wardrobe department, to provide clothing or sets of clothes.
Unexceptional or unimportant; blah.
At all, absolutely, whatsoever.
definitions.example: There is no point whatever in going on with this discussion.

definitions.definition: No matter what; for any
definitions.example: Whatever he does, he will still lose the game.

definitions.definition: (relative) Anything; sometimes used to indicate that the speaker does not care about options.
definitions.example: I’ll do whatever I can.

definitions.definition: (dismissive) A holophrastic expression used discourteously to indicate that the speaker does not consider the matter worthy of further discussion.
definitions.example: "For the last time, brush your teeth!" "Whatever!"

definitions.definition: A lash; a pliant, flexible instrument, such as a rod (commonly of cane or rattan) or a plaited or braided rope or thong (commonly of leather) used to create a sharp "crack" sound for directing or herding animals.
definitions.example: I had to use the whip to get the sheep's attention.

definitions.definition: A blow administered with a whip.
A whipper-in.
A member of a political party who is in charge of enforcing the party's policies in votes.
definitions.example: I was going to vote against the bill, but the party whip came to see me and made it clear I needed to vote for it.

definitions.definition: (UK politics, with definite article) A document distributed weekly to MPs by party whips informing them of upcoming votes in parliament.
Whipped cream.
definitions.example: Did you want to add some whip to your coffee, ma'am?

definitions.definition: A purchase in which one block is used to gain a 2:1 mechanical advantage.
A mode of personal motorized transportation; an automobile, all makes and models including motorcycles, excluding public transportation.
definitions.example: Come on, let's take my whip so we can get there in time.

definitions.definition: A move in which one player transfers momentum to another.
A whipping motion; a thrashing about.
definitions.example: I was startled by the whip of the rope when it finally snapped.

definitions.definition: The quality of being whiplike or flexible; suppleness, as of the shaft of a golf club.
Any of various pieces that operate with a quick vibratory motion
A coach driver; a coachman.
To hit with a whip.
definitions.example: The rider whipped the horse.

definitions.definition: (by extension) To hit with any flexible object.
definitions.example: I whipped her with a newspaper.

definitions.definition: To defeat, as in a contest or game.
To mix in a rapid aerating fashion, especially food.
definitions.example: to whip eggs or cream

definitions.definition: To urge into action or obedience.
definitions.example: He whipped the department into shape.

definitions.definition: To enforce a member voting in accordance with party policy.
To bind the end of a rope with twine or other small stuff to prevent its unlaying: fraying or unravelling.
To hoist or purchase by means of a whip.
To sew lightly; specifically, to form (a fabric) into gathers by loosely overcasting the rolled edge and drawing up the thread.
definitions.example: to whip a ruffle

definitions.definition: To throw or kick an object at a high velocity.
definitions.example: He whipped the ball at me.

definitions.definition: To fish a body of water especially by making repeated casts.
To snap back and forth like a whip.
definitions.example: The pennants whipped in the wind.

definitions.definition: To move very fast.
definitions.example: The wind whipped through the valley.

definitions.definition: To move (something) very fast; often with up, out, etc.
To transfer momentum from one skater to another.
To lash with sarcasm, abuse, etc.
To thrash; to beat out, as grain, by striking.
definitions.example: to whip wheat

definitions.definition: To make weaker or less strong.
To become weaker or less strong.
An instance or process of loss of strength.
A structural principle of mathematical logic that states that the hypotheses of any derived fact may be freely extended with additional assumptions.
The act or style of writing.
The operation of storing data, as in memory or onto disk.
definitions.example: How many writes per second can this hard disk handle?

definitions.definition: To form letters, words or symbols on a surface in order to communicate.
definitions.example: The pupil wrote his name on the paper.

definitions.definition: To be the author of (a book, article, poem, etc.).
definitions.example: My uncle writes newspaper articles for The Herald.

definitions.definition: To send written information to.
definitions.example: (UK) Please write to me when you get there.

definitions.definition: To show (information, etc) in written form.
definitions.example: The due day of the homework is written in the syllabus.

definitions.definition: To be an author.
definitions.example: I write for a living.

definitions.definition: (with to) To record data mechanically or electronically.
definitions.example: The computer writes to the disk faster than it reads from it.

definitions.definition: (of an exam, a document, etc.) To fill in, to complete using words.
definitions.example: I was very anxious to know my score after I wrote the test.

definitions.definition: To impress durably; to imprint; to engrave.
definitions.example: truth written on the heart

definitions.definition: To make known by writing; to record; to prove by one's own written testimony; often used reflexively.
To sell (an option or other derivative).
A day of the week except those which form the weekend; that is:
Any day of the week (Monday through Sunday).
Having a temperature slightly higher than usual, but still pleasant; mildly hot.
definitions.example: The tea is still warm.

definitions.definition: Caring and friendly, of relations to another person.
definitions.example: We have a warm friendship.

definitions.definition: Having a color in the red-orange-yellow part of the visible electromagnetic spectrum.
Close, often used in the context of a game in which "warm" and "cold" are used to indicate nearness to the goal.
Fresh, of a scent; still able to be traced.
Communicating a sense of comfort, ease, or pleasantness

definitions.definition: Ardent, zealous.

definitions.definition: Well off as to property, or in good circumstances; rich.
Requiring arduous effort.
To make or keep warm.
To become warm, to heat up.

definitions.definition: To favour increasingly.

definitions.definition: To become ardent or animated.

definitions.definition: To make engaged or earnest; to interest; to engage; to excite ardor or zeal in; to enliven.
To beat or spank.
Of, pertaining to, or characteristic of witches; witchlike.

definitions.definition: Something, such as an advanced technology, that seems almost magical.
A person who is actively engaged in battle, conflict or warfare; a soldier or combatant.
A person who is aggressively, courageously, or energetically involved in an activity, such as athletics.
A smell; especially a pleasant spicy or fragrant one.
definitions.example: I love the aroma of cinnamon.

To neutralize alkalis, as to acidify sugar
To sour, to embitter.
Someone who supports or heralds the establishment of a utopia.
Ideal but often impractical; visionary.

definitions.definition: Of or pertaining to or resembling a utopia.

definitions.definition: An undergarment worn beneath a shirt, often collarless and sleeveless.

Underpants (boxers, briefs, panties, etc) and often bras.

Of a deep yellowish-orange colour.

Connected sequence of people or things.

To create a trainer for; to apply cheats to (a game).

A partially enclosed glass container for displaying plants, especially plants that need high humidity.

The knights collectively, the body of knights.
A dense uncultivated tract of trees and undergrowth, larger than woods.
Any dense collection or amount.

A graph with no cycles; i.e., a graph made up of trees.

The colour forest green.
To cover an area with trees.
The part of the face above the eyebrows and below the hairline.

The upper part of a mobile phone, above the screen.
Unspoiled; still with its original purity; uncorrupted or unsullied.
Primitive, pertaining to the earliest state of something.
Perfect.
To clothe with, or as with, a vestment, or garment; to dress; to robe; to cover, surround, or encompass closely.
To clothe with authority, power, etc.; to put in possession; to invest; to furnish; to endow; followed by with and the thing conferred.
definitions.example: to vest a court with power to try cases of life and death

definitions.definition: To place or give into the possession or discretion of some person or authority; to commit to another; with in before the possessor.
definitions.example: The power of life and death is vested in the king, or in the courts.

definitions.definition: To invest; to put.
definitions.example: to vest money in goods, land, or houses

definitions.definition: To clothe with possession; also, to give a person an immediate fixed right of present or future enjoyment of.
definitions.example: an estate is vested in possession

definitions.definition: (of an inheritance or a trust fund) To devolve upon the person currently entitled when a prior interest has ended.
definitions.example: Upon the death of the Sovereign the Crown automatically vests in the next heir without the need of coronation or other formality.

definitions.definition: (financial) To become vested, to become permanent.
definitions.example: My pension vests at the end of the month and then I can take it with me when I quit.

definitions.definition: Settled, fixed or absolute, with no contingencies.
Dressed or clothed, especially in vestments.
definitions.example: The Pope, vested in mitre and cope, is greeted by a newly created Cardinal.

definitions.definition: In a vague manner.
definitions.example: He looked vaguely around the room, as if lost.

definitions.definition: Loosely; more or less; somewhat.
Being the winner in a contest, struggle, war, etc.
Of or expressing a sense of victory or triumph.
definitions.example: The team were met with a victorious cheer.

definitions.definition: Any of a specific group of organic compounds essential in small quantities for healthy human growth, metabolism, development, and body function; found in minute amounts in plant and animal foods or sometimes produced synthetically; deficiencies of specific vitamins produce specific disorders.

A white liquid produced by the mammary glands of female mammals to nourish their young. From certain animals, especially cows, it is also called dairy milk and is a common food for humans as a beverage or used to produce various dairy products such as butter, cheese, and yogurt.
A white (or whitish) liquid obtained from a vegetable source such as almonds, coconuts, oats, rice, and/or soy beans. Also called non-dairy milk.
An individual serving of milk.
definitions.example: Table three ordered three milks. (Formally: The guests at table three ordered three glasses of milk.)

definitions.definition: An individual portion of milk, such as found in a creamer, for tea and coffee.
definitions.example: I take my tea with two milk(s) and two sugar.

definitions.definition: The ripe, undischarged spat of an oyster.
Semen.
A distinguishing blotch of color.
A mottled coloration or pattern.
definitions.example: The most common symptom is a mild mottle on the youngest leaves of infected plants.

definitions.definition: To mark with blotches of different color, or shades of color, as if stained; to spot; to maculate.
A feeling of extreme happiness or cheerfulness, especially related to the acquisition or expectation of something good.
definitions.example: They will be a source of strength and joy in your life.

definitions.definition: Anything that causes such a feeling.
definitions.example: the joys and demands of parenthood

definitions.definition: Luck or success; a positive outcome.
The sign or exhibition of joy; gaiety; merriment; festivity.
To heat excessively.
To become excessively hot.
A situation where something is overheated.
To move back and forth repeatedly and somewhat loosely.
definitions.example: The flag waved in the gentle breeze.

definitions.definition: To move one’s hand back and forth (generally above the shoulders) in greeting or departure.
(metonymic) To call attention to, or give a direction or command to, by a waving motion, as of the hand; to signify by waving; to beckon; to signal; to indicate.
definitions.example: I waved goodbye from across the room.

definitions.definition: To have an undulating or wavy form.
To raise into inequalities of surface; to give an undulating form or surface to.
To produce waves to the hair.
To swing and miss at a pitch.
definitions.example: Jones waves at strike one.

definitions.definition: To cause to move back and forth repeatedly.
definitions.example: The starter waved the flag to begin the race.

definitions.definition: (metonymic) To signal (someone or something) with a waving movement.
To fluctuate; to waver; to be in an unsettled state.
To move like a wave, or by floating; to waft.
Something that can be worn; an item of clothing.
Ellipsis of wearable computer
Able to be worn.
Any small luminous dot appearing in the cloudless portion of the night sky, especially with a fixed location relative to other such dots.
A luminous celestial body, made up of plasma (particularly hydrogen and helium) and having a spherical shape. Depending on context the sun may or may not be included.
A concave polygon with regular, pointy protrusions and indentations, generally with five or six points.
An actor in a leading role.
definitions.example: Many Hollywood stars attended the launch party.

definitions.definition: An exceptionally talented or famous person, often in a specific field; a celebrity.
definitions.example: His teacher tells us he is a star pupil.

definitions.definition: An asterisk (*).
A symbol used to rate hotels, films, etc. with a higher number of stars denoting better quality.

A simple dance, or part of a dance, where a group of four dancers each put their right or left hand in the middle and turn around in a circle. You call them right-hand stars or left-hand stars, depending on the hand which is in the middle.
A planet supposed to influence one's destiny.
definitions.example: What's in the stars for you today? Find out in our horoscope.

definitions.definition: A star-shaped ornament worn on the breast to indicate rank or honour.
A composition of combustible matter used in the heading of rockets, in mines, etc., which, exploding in the air, presents a starlike appearance.
To appear as a featured performer or headliner, especially in an entertainment program.
To feature (a performer or a headliner), especially in a movie or an entertainment program.
To mark with a star or asterisk.
To set or adorn with stars, or bright, radiating bodies; to bespangle.
To shine like a star.
(with "the") Outer space.
Not fair, just or right.
definitions.example: The solution was very unjust.

definitions.definition: A non-native person who comes to a country from another country in order to permanently settle there.
A plant or animal that establishes itself in an area where it previously did not exist.
A device, usually hinged with a striking plate, used for knocking on a door.
A person who knocks.
A critic; one who disparages.
A person who knocks (denigrates) something.
(usually in the plural) A woman's breasts.
(especially Cardigan) A dwarf, goblin, or sprite imagined to dwell in mines and to indicate the presence of ore by knocking.
A mechanical device in a pinball table that produces a loud percussive noise.
A person who is strikingly handsome or otherwise admirable; a stunner.
A large cockroach, especially Blaberus giganteus, of semitropical America, which is able to produce a loud knocking sound.
A large, boulder-shaped outcrop of bedrock in an otherwise low-lying landscape, chiefly associated with a mélange.
One who defaults on payment of a wager.
A decorative Native American object in the form of a hoop and net with attachments such as feathers, traditionally believed by the Ojibwa to “filter out” bad dreams.
An evil creature.
(with article) The chief devil; Satan.

A fictional image of a man, usually red or orange in skin color; with a set of horns on his head, a pointed goatee and a long tail and carrying a pitchfork; that represents evil and portrayed to children in an effort to discourage bad behavior.
The bad part of the conscience; the opposite to the angel.
definitions.example: The devil in me wants to let him suffer.

definitions.definition: A wicked or naughty person, or one who harbors reckless, spirited energy, especially in a mischievous way; usually said of a young child.
definitions.example: Those two kids are devils in a toy store.

definitions.definition: A thing that is awkward or difficult to understand or do.
definitions.example: That math problem was a devil.

definitions.definition: (with an article, as an intensifier) Hell.
definitions.example: She is having a devil of a time fixing it.

definitions.definition: A person, especially a man; used to express a particular opinion of him, usually in the phrases poor devil and lucky devil.
A dust devil.
(Christian Science) An evil or erring entity.
(in compounds) A barren, unproductive and unused area.
definitions.example: devil strip

definitions.definition: A dish, as a bone with the meat, broiled and excessively peppered; a grill with Cayenne pepper.
A machine for tearing or cutting rags, cotton, etc.
A Tasmanian devil.
An endurance event where riders who fall behind are periodically eliminated.
To make like a devil; to invest with the character of a devil.
To annoy or bother.
To work as a ‘devil’; to work for a lawyer or writer without fee or recognition.
To prepare (food) with spices, making it spicy:
A person or entity engaged in the creation or improvement of certain classes of products.
A real estate developer; a person or company who prepares a parcel of land for sale, or creates structures on that land.
A film developer; a person who uses chemicals to create photographs from photograph negatives.
A liquid used in the chemical processing of traditional photos.
(dyeing) A reagent used to produce an ingrain color by its action upon some substance on the fiber.
A software developer; a person or company who creates or modifies computer software.
Subject to death; mortal.
Causing death; lethal.
Aiming or willing to destroy; implacable; desperately hostile.
definitions.example: deadly enemies

definitions.definition: Very accurate (of aiming with a bow, firearm, etc.).
Very boring.
Excellent, awesome, cool.
Fatally, mortally.
In a way which suggests death.
definitions.example: Her face suddenly became deadly white.

definitions.definition: Extremely.
The disaccharide sugar of milk and dairy products, C12H22O11, a product of glucose and galactose used as a food and in medicinal compounds.
A cetacean, Delphinapterus leucas, found in the Arctic Ocean.
A fish, Huso huso, found in the Caspian Sea and the Black Sea, that is a source of caviar.
A person of major significance, accomplishment or acclaim.
definitions.example: Newton and Einstein are two of the greats of the history of science.

definitions.definition: The main division in a pipe organ, usually the loudest division.
(in combinations such as "two-greats", "three-greats" etc.) An instance of the word "great" signifying an additional generation in phrases expressing family relationships.
definitions.example: My three-greats grandmother.

definitions.definition: Relatively large in scale, size, extent, number (i.e. having many parts or members) or duration (i.e. relatively long); very big.
definitions.example: A great storm is approaching our shores.

definitions.definition: Of larger size or more importance than others of its kind.
definitions.example: the great auk

definitions.definition: (qualifying nouns of family relationship) Involving more generations than the qualified word implies — as many extra generations as repetitions of the word great (from 1510s). [see Derived terms]
definitions.example: great-grandfather, great-great-grandfather, great-great-great-grandfather

definitions.definition: (followed by 'with') Pregnant; large with young; full of.
definitions.example: great with child

definitions.definition: (except with 'friend' and similar words such as 'mate','buddy') Intimate; familiar.
Extreme or more than usual.
definitions.example: great worry

definitions.definition: Of significant importance or consequence; important.
definitions.example: a great decision

definitions.definition: (applied to actions, thoughts and feelings) Arising from or possessing idealism; admirable; superior; commanding; heroic; illustrious; eminent.
definitions.example: a great deed

definitions.definition: Impressive or striking.
definitions.example: a great show of wealth

definitions.definition: Much in use; favoured.
definitions.example: Poetry was a great convention of the Romantic era.

definitions.definition: (applied to persons) Endowed with extraordinary powers; of exceptional talents or achievements; uncommonly gifted; able to accomplish vast results; remarkable; strong; powerful; mighty; noble.
definitions.example: a great hero, scholar, genius, philosopher, writer etc.

definitions.definition: Title referring to an important leader.
definitions.example: Alexander the Great

definitions.definition: Doing or exemplifying (a characteristic or pursuit) on a large scale; active or enthusiastic.
definitions.example: He's not a great one for reading.

definitions.definition: (often followed by 'at') Skilful or adroit.
definitions.example: You are great at singing.

definitions.definition: Very good; excellent; wonderful; fantastic (from 1848).
definitions.example: Dinner was great.

definitions.definition: Intensifying a word or expression, used in mild oaths.
definitions.example: Great Scott!

definitions.definition: Very well (in a very satisfactory manner).
definitions.example: Those mechanical colored pencils work great because they don't have to be sharpened.

definitions.definition: Expression of gladness and content about something.
definitions.example: Great! Thanks for the wonderful work.

definitions.definition: Sarcastic inversion thereof.
definitions.example: Oh, great! I just dumped all 500 sheets of the manuscript all over and now I have to put them back in order.

definitions.definition: A heavy garment worn over other clothes, for protection from cold or weather.
To apply an exterior coating to.
To have rightful possession of (property, goods or capital); to have legal title to.
definitions.example: I own this car.

definitions.definition: To have recognized political sovereignty over a place, territory, as distinct from the ordinary connotation of property ownership.
definitions.example: The United States owns Point Roberts by the terms of the Treaty of Oregon.

definitions.definition: To defeat or embarrass; to overwhelm.
definitions.example: I will own my enemies.

definitions.definition: To virtually or figuratively enslave.
To defeat, dominate, or be above, also spelled pwn.
To illicitly obtain superuser or root access to a computer system, thereby having access to all of the user files on that system; pwn.
To grant; give.
To admit, concede, grant, allow, acknowledge, confess; not to deny.
To admit; concede; acknowledge.
To take responsibility for.
To answer to.
To recognise; acknowledge.
definitions.example: to own one as a son

definitions.definition: To claim as one's own.
To confess.
An English unit of land area (symbol: a. or ac.) originally denoting a day's plowing for a yoke of oxen, now standardized as 4,840 square yards or 4,046.86 square meters.
Any of various similar units of area in other systems.
(usually in the plural) A wide expanse.
definitions.example: I like my new house - there’s acres of space!

definitions.definition: (usually in the plural) A large quantity.
A field.
The acre's breadth by the length, English units of length equal to the statute dimensions of the acre: 22 yds (≈20 m) by 220 yds (≈200 m).
A duel fought between individual Scots and Englishmen in the borderlands.
